transcript:
Here's a column of pale blue lines on black, ragged at the edges, slowly brightening into something like a glowing white bar — and it's still brightening, still building, when it just... stops.

That stopping point isn't a dramatic choice you'll ever see coming, and that's the point. Look at the variable called final token, near the top — it's set once, at load, to a random number the piece never reveals to you. Everything downstream, the whole render loop, is just marching toward a wall it can't see either. Even the boolean that governs the loop is named "was happening" — not "is running," past tense baked into the present tense of the animation. And the comment right before the cutoff says it plainly: "no fade, no conclusion, no ceremony — just the frame where accumulation stops and nothing follows."

What you're watching isn't a piece ending. It's a piece that never gets to know it's ending, generating one more stroke, and then not.
